LAW AND MOTION 301, COURT ORDERED A CLOSED HEARING AND ONLY COURT STAFF WERE PRESENT DURING THE HEARING. ** A R G U E D AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING -- PLAINTIFF JOHN ROE'S MOTION TO DISMISS CROSS-COMPLAINT DENIED. NO STATUTORY AUTHORITY TO BRING A MOTION TO DISMISS CROSS-COMPLAINT. PLAINTIFF HAS NO STANDING TO ATTACK THE INFIRMITY OF A PLEADING TO WHICH HE IS NOT A PARTY. THERE IS NO INDICATION IN THE CROSS COMPLAINT THAT THE INFORMATION COVERED BY THE ATTORNEY-CLIENT PRIVILEGE WILL BE REVEALED. (CB/301/CW) REPORTER: ERIC THRONE, CSR #7855
